title: Business Financial Advisory
summary: Practical guidance for small business owners and entrepreneurs — cash flow, separating personal and business finances, and planning for growth.
audience: Small business owners, entrepreneurs, and early-stage founders.
overview: Business Financial Advisory is practical, educational guidance for people running a small business or early-stage venture — help understanding cash flow, separating personal and business money, and building simple habits that support growth. It's a complement to formal accounting, tax, and audit services, not a replacement for them.
whoItsFor: You're running a small business and personal and business money are mixed together
You're not sure how to read your own cash flow
You're planning to grow and want a practical financial footing first
You want plain guidance, not a formal audit or compliance service
whatYoullLearn: How to separate personal and business finances properly
How to understand and plan around your business's cash flow
What financial habits actually support growth, not just survival
Simple record-keeping habits that make future decisions easier

process.title: Share your business situation
process.description: Where the business is today, and what's prompting the conversation.

process.title: Identify the highest-impact areas
process.description: Usually one or two specific things worth focusing on first, not everything at once.

process.title: Work through a practical plan
process.description: Concrete, doable steps built around your business, not a generic template.

faq.question: Is this the same as hiring an accountant?
faq.answer: No — this is complementary financial education and advisory, not a replacement for licensed accounting, tax, or audit services. Many readers use both.

faq.question: Can you help with a business plan?
faq.answer: The financial side of a business plan — cash flow, basic projections, financial habits — is within scope; broader business strategy is not the focus of this service.

faq.question: Do you work with registered and unregistered businesses?
faq.answer: Yes — the fundamentals covered apply at any stage, including very early or informal ventures.

faq.question: Is this only for businesses based in Accra?
faq.answer: No — sessions can be arranged remotely; location isn't a barrier.

faq.question: What if my business has irregular income?
faq.answer: That's a common reality for small businesses in Ghana, and it's factored directly into the guidance rather than treated as an edge case.

faq.question: How technical do I need to be?
faq.answer: Not technical at all — this is explained in plain language, with no assumption of accounting or finance background.

faq.question: Do you provide legal or tax advice?
faq.answer: No — this service is financial education and advisory only. For legal or tax matters, we'd point you toward a licensed professional.

complianceNote: Robayer WealthLab provides financial education and advisory, not licensed accounting, tax, or legal advice.
